title: Investing in gold alternatives — digital, ETFs, mutual funds or SGBs? Check if they can be converted to physical gold
summary: Are you investing in gold alternatives — digital gold, gold ETFs, gold mutual funds or sovereign gold bonds (SGBs)? Check here to see if these options can all be converted to physical gold and how they are taxed in India.

title: What is digital gold? How can you buy it, and can it be converted to physical gold?
summary: Digital gold allows online investment in physical gold without immediate possession. It is also backed by real gold stored in secure vaults but not regulated by Sebi or RBI. Details here.

title: National Pension Scheme: Maharashtra makes revised NPS opt-in for employees — Check deadline, minimum pay, other details
summary: Maharashtra government has made its revised National Pension Scheme optional for current employees and given till 31 December to opt-in. Here's a look at how pension will be calculated, the minimum monthly payout and years of service required to claim.&nbsp;

title: Small savings scheme interest rates in May 2026: Returns on PPF, SSY, SCSS, NSC and others compared
summary: Small savings scheme interest rates in May 2026 highlight stable government-backed returns across PPF, SSY, SCSS and NSC. These are ideal for conservative investors who seek to balance volatility and tax efficiency.

title: How much gold can you legally keep at home? Rules explained amid PM Modi's appeal to avoid buying the yellow metal
summary: Prime Minister Modi urged citizens to avoid gold purchases for a year to reduce non-essential imports. India's gold ownership rules allow certain limits on gold jewellery during tax raids. Here's all you need to know.&nbsp;

title: 8th CPC: Market volatility hurting payout? Central govt employees representative demands assured pension — What we know
summary: A representative body for central government employees has requested the 8th CPC to ensure a minimum payout under NPS to counter market volatility. A memorandum detailing the demands has been submitted to the panel.

title: Tenant refusing to vacate house in Delhi? Key legal provisions for eviction and rights of landlords explained
summary: If a tenant refuses to vacate a house in Delhi, there are key provisions under the Delhi Rent Control Act that can protect the landlord's rights. They include eviction, legal remedies, and Supreme Court rulings for landlords and tenants.

title: SCSS maturity rules explained: Can senior citizens extend their account's tenure?
summary: The Senior Citizens Savings Scheme offers retirees a popular fixed-income investment with government backing and quarterly payouts. Eligible individuals can invest, extend their accounts multiple times, and earn an 8.2% annual interest rate.&nbsp;

title: Gen Z investors are rewriting the rules of investing
summary: Gen Z has become the country's fastest-growing investor cohort, with under-30s now comprising 38% of the National Stock Exchange's base, but experts warn their decisions are driven more by social media, peer pressure and post-covid return euphoria than by sound financial strategy.
